predicted_external_diagram.situation: Alex avoids being around people when things get tough to avoid questions and judgment.
predicted_external_diagram.automatic_thought: If I let my guard down, people will see the real me and not like what they find.
predicted_external_diagram.behavior: Alex withdraws from others and turns to substances to escape feelings of inadequacy and fear.

ground_truth_external_diagram.situation: Alex's cousin invited him to attend his upcoming wedding.
ground_truth_external_diagram.automatic_thought: It will be stressful and negative; people will ask me questions I cannot answer or don't want to answer; my mum would be ashamed of me and critical of me as always; They don't want me there anyway, nobody likes me in this family.
ground_truth_external_diagram.emotion: anxious, worried, fearful, scared, tense; sad, down, lonely, unhappy
ground_truth_external_diagram.behavior: Ignored the invitation and did not respond to the RSVP request. Ignored phone calls from family.

predicted_internal_diagram.relevant_history: The client reports turning to substances as a way to escape feelings of inadequacy and fear, and has experienced moments of feeling heard and validated during group sessions in rehab. The client feels pressure from family expectations, particularly from their mother, which contributes to feelings of being judged and not good enough. The client has been involved in legal matters and custody issues, which add stress and a sense of fighting for control in their life.
predicted_internal_diagram.core_beliefs: I am not good enough | I am not worth the effort to change | I am unworthy | I am destined to keep playing this role that everyone else has set for me
predicted_internal_diagram.intermediate_beliefs: If I let my guard down, people will see the real me and not like what they find. | If I express what I want and it doesn’t align with what my family expects, then I feel like I’m somehow failing or being selfish. | The client believes that wanting anything for themselves is somehow wrong or selfish.
predicted_internal_diagram.coping_strategies: The client copes by withdrawing from others to avoid judgment and criticism, turning to substances to escape feelings of inadequacy and fear, and maintaining a facade to present themselves in the best light. The client also considers using breathing exercises, grounding techniques, and affirmations to stay grounded and challenge negative thoughts.

ground_truth_internal_diagram.relevant_history: The patient has a history of substance abuse and has been through rehab to overcome it. He has had issues with his family, particularly with his mother, where he has felt rejected and emotionally neglected. He has struggled with obesity since childhood, which has affected his self-esteem and body image. He also has a history of being victimized and bullied due to his weight.
ground_truth_internal_diagram.core_beliefs: I am trapped. | I am out of control. | I am unlovable. | I am undesirable, unwanted.
ground_truth_internal_diagram.intermediate_beliefs:
Helpless: I'm just not very good at handling stress and I have poor self-control, which is why I need to not put myself in stressful situations. 
 Unlovable: If I show my true self, people will reject me the way my mother rejected me.
Depressive variant: Helpless: There's nothing I can do to change my situation. I cannot control myself. 
Unlovable: I don't deserve to be happy, so why even try to stay clean?
ground_truth_internal_diagram.coping_strategies: The patient has adopted avoidance as a coping strategy by distancing himself from his family to reduce exposure to negativity and conflict. He has started scheduling pleasant activities and planning his day ahead of time in order to maintain a sense of control over his emotions and circumstances.
